subject: Improve responsiveness of tabs in job view
description:
## Motivation
When opening big openQA test jobs with many test modules (e.g. ltp_syscalls) one see "Loading test modules…" wheel. That blocks clicking on the other linear tabs (one of: Logs & Assets, Settings, Investigation, Comments, Next & previous results) until whole test is loaded (up to 1 min). Loading test modules should be possible to cancel by clicking to the new tab.

## Acceptance criteria
* **AC1:** "Big jobs" do not prevent to switch to other test details tabs while loading (during the AJAX request and during client-side processing).
* **AC2:** Test details for small jobs are as easily accessible as before.

## Suggestions
* Some kind of async "switch tab" handling to abort the loading of modules or continue loading the modules in the background or some fancy incremental loading of details
* Do DOM-insertions in chunks allowing the event loop to run in between or alternatively for "big jobs" only render everything on request, e.g. a button "this job is huge, click here to load details"
* Run some big LTP test locally to have data to play with

journals.author: okurz
journals.notes: tracking as tasks/025_poo196991_improve_responsiveness_of_tabs_in_job_details_view in (to be checked out) feature/025_poo196991_improve_responsiveness_of_tabs_in_job_details_view

journals.author: okurz
journals.notes: Accessing https://openqa.opensuse.org also takes very long to render results due to the inclusion of job group 132 "Investigation" that has more than 10k jobs.. Accessing a page like https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/overview?distri=aeon&distri=microos&distri=openqa&distri=opensuse&version=Tumbleweed&build=&groupid=22&groupid=107&groupid=2&groupid=132 shows a warning "Only 2000 results included, please narrow down your search parameters. " so we already limit the impact of "too big results", that's good. We should consider to use the same or a very similar approach for the job group and index pages.
